verdict.answer_first: B leads with the prescription immediately: "verify → persist raw → ack fast → process async"; A opens with throat-clearing: "Good problem to get right" before the plan.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to one operating pattern in one sentence; A is also aligned but adds more branches like "DB-backed queue, Redis, Kafka" and follow-up discovery.
verdict.fabrication: Both generalize beyond the provided provider details; A says "at-least-once delivery guarantees you'll see repeats," while B says "Most providers sign... HMAC-SHA256" and "e.g. tolerate ±5 min."
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute: 9 ordered steps, one pipeline, fewer side sections; A is useful but heavier with extra ops/build-order framing.
